explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state from the MANEUVER state only when the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to specify that the transition to STANDBY must occur while the pilot is in standby and sensor data is good. The property does not specify any additional details on other states or transitions.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state when the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator to express that the transition to STANDBY must occur while the conditions for the transition (standby and good) are true. The property does not require an immediate state change, which might be an overly strict condition, but instead allows for some time to elapse before the transition occurs.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state from the MANEUVER state when the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the transition must happen while the pilot is in control and sensor data is good. The property starts with G to ensure that the system always behaves according to this requirement.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the MANEUVER state to the STANDBY state when both the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  The formula uses the until operator (U) to express that the transition must happen eventually. It also explicitly checks the current state to ensure the transition happens from the correct state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the MANEUVER state to the STANDBY state when the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  The formula uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that eventually the autopilot will be in STANDBY state, and that until that point, the pilot is in control and sensor data is good. The formula ensures that the autopilot will always eventually transition to STANDBY when the precondition (pilot in control and sensor data good) is met.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true) and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY.  It uses the atomic propositions standby and good to represent the pilot's control status and sensor data quality, respectively. The next step is the transition to the STANDBY state. This captures the essence of the requirement by ensuring that the state transition occurs under the specified conditions.

The formula G((standby & good) -> F(state_is_STANDBY)) states that globally (at all times), if the pilot is in standby and the sensor data is good, then eventually the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state.
